description: FinalizationRegistry.prototype.unregister OOB Argument Read — args[0] Used Without Bounds Check. Bug 1987290 (sec-high, csectype-bounds). The native implementation of FinalizationRegistry.prototype.unregister in js/src/builtin/FinalizationRegistryObject.cpp uses `args[0]` to grab the unregister-token argument instead of the bounds-checking `args.get(0)` helper. When JS code calls `registry.unregister()` with no arguments, JS::CallArgs::operator[] asserts `i < argc_` and the underlying read steps past the end of the call frame's outgoing argument area into adjacent stack memory. In a debug build the MOZ_ASSERT at js/CallArgs.h:213 fires; in an ASAN build the read tags as a stack OOB or stack-buffer-underflow that ASAN reports as a deadly signal. The bug was a regression from Bug 1863140 Part 3 (Phabricator D263113) that refactored the dispatcher and dropped the `get()` use. Fix: 2b8155c2c4c299955d4ae81423992ed56a838aee 'Bug 1987290 - Part 1: Use get() instead of operator[] to get arguments in FinalizationRegistryObject::unregister' (Tooru Fujisawa, 2025-09-08); the test landed in Part 2 (dad31486e264). PoC is the two-line idiom `new FinalizationRegistry(()=>{}).unregister()` reported externally on Bugzilla.

fixes.description: Bug 1987290 - Part 1: Use get() instead of operator[] to get
